title: Erdős–Hajnal Problem on Locally Ramsey Graphs
statement_text: Over all $n$-vertex graphs $G$, what is the smallest possible value of $m$ for which any $m$ vertices of $G$ contain both a clique and an independent set of size $\log n$?
context_text: Erdős and Hajnal raised this problem in the early 1990s, asking for the smallest $m = m_G(k)$ such that every $m$-vertex subset of an $n$-vertex graph $G$ contains both a clique and an independent set of size $k$. For $k = \log n$ the random graph gives an upper bound of order $\sqrt{n}$, and understanding this quantity is closely tied to the structure of Ramsey graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves (Theorem 1) the existence of an $n$-vertex graph $G$ with $m_G(\log n) \leq 2^{2(\log\log n)^{1/2}+o(1)}$, a twofold sub-polynomial improvement over the $O(\sqrt{n})$ upper bound coming from the random graph; also gives a general bound in Theorem 2 and determines $m_n(k) = \Theta(k\log n)$ for small $k$ in Proposition 3.
notes: PDF source. Problem raised 'in the early 90's' per the introduction; exact year unknown so left null. Reference [13] is the Erdős–Hajnal paper.
paper_title: Large cliques and independent sets all over the place

title: Erdős Question: does $m_G(\log n) = (\log n)^3$ exist?
statement_text: Does there exist an $n$-vertex graph $G$ with $m_G(\log n) = (\log n)^3$?
context_text: Erdős singled out the case $k = \log n$ and asked whether graphs with $m_G(\log n) = (\log n)^3$ exist. A positive answer would yield $((\log n)^3, \log n)$-locally Ramsey graphs very different from the random graph. The paper states explicitly that this question remains open; the companion lower bound $m_G(\log n) \geq \Omega((\log n)^3/\log\log n)$ from reference [4] gives partial support to Erdős's choice of parameters.
paper_contribution: Improves the best known upper bound from $O(\sqrt{n})$ to $2^{2(\log\log n)^{1/2}+o(1)}$ (Theorem 1), narrowing the gap but leaving the specific $(\log n)^3$ target unresolved.
notes: PDF source. Attributed to Erdős alone via reference [13] (an Erdős–Hajnal paper). Explicitly marked open: 'This question remains open.'
paper_title: Large cliques and independent sets all over the place
